headline: GOLD TESTS $4,800 RESISTANCE as dollar hits six-week low on softer U.S. PPI data
snippet: Gold climbed to test $4,800/oz on Tuesday as softer-than-expected U.S. PPI data dragged the dollar to its lowest level in six weeks, with easing U.S.-Iran tensions providing additional tailwind. The metal remains roughly 14% below its January 29 all-time high of $5,595 but has recovered strongly from March lows — its highest print since March 18.

headline: SILVER SQUEEZE: white metal surges 5%+ as solar and EV demand ignites structural industrial bull
snippet: Silver surged more than 5% in Tuesday's session to trade above $79/oz as industrial buyers — including solar panel manufacturers and EV automakers — have begun securing direct supply contracts with miners, bypassing spot markets and tightening available physical supply. With global solar PV capacity forecast at 665 GW in 2026 consuming ~120-125 Moz of silver annually, analysts argue the price floor has permanently risen.

headline: NEVGOLD HITS 0.34% Sb, 0.41 g/t Au over 12.5m at Nevada gold leach pads — antimony MRE due Q2
snippet: NevGold Corp. (TSXV: NAU) reported consistent antimony-gold intercepts from sonic drilling on historic gold leach pads at its Limousine Butte Project in Nevada, including 0.34% Sb and 0.41 g/t Au over 12.5m, 0.31% Sb and 0.43 g/t Au over 13.7m, and 0.33% Sb and 0.55 g/t Au over 11.0m. A maiden antimony-gold Mineral Resource Estimate is targeted for Q2-2026 with a path to near-term antimony production by 2027.

headline: CHINA gold ETFs post RECORD US$8.5B quarterly inflows; PBoC buys gold for 17th straight month
snippet: Chinese gold ETFs attracted a record RMB 59 billion (US$8.5B) in Q1-2026 inflows, lifting total holdings to 298 tonnes and AUM to RMB 304 billion, as retail investors, wholesalers, and the People's Bank of China all bought the dip. The PBoC added 5 tonnes in March — its 17th consecutive monthly purchase — bringing reserves to 2,313 tonnes, now 9% of total foreign reserves.

headline: Gold's April surge is a MONETARY WARNING SIGNAL — not just a safe-haven trade, analysts argue
snippet: A MarketMinute analysis argues gold's April surge mirrors the structural breaks seen in 2009 and 2020, contending that investors are now treating bullion as a hedge against system-level fiscal and monetary risk rather than short-term volatility. With gold up over 25% since the start of 2025 and debt monetization concerns intensifying, the piece warns that a return to the $4,300-4,500 range could attract institutional buyers who are still underweight.

headline: COPPER SMASHES $6/LB: the one mining ETF that gives pure-play exposure to the move
snippet: Copper surged above $6/lb (approximately $13,200/tonne) on strong structural demand from EV batteries, AI data centre buildouts, and grid electrification — up more than 30% year-over-year — with 24/7 Wall St. identifying a pure-play mining ETF for investors who want direct copper exposure rather than diversified major miners. Copper futures gave back some gains Monday on tariff uncertainty but the longer-term structural deficit thesis remains intact.

headline: CHINA silently stockpiling silver: 790+ tonnes imported in Q1 as solar manufacturing drains supply
snippet: The weekly Gold SWOT column from U.S. Global Investors reports that China imported more than 790 tonnes of silver in early 2026, driven overwhelmingly by its solar panel manufacturing sector — making China's industrial appetite the primary structural reason global silver supply cannot keep pace with consumption. Analysts note that 70-80% of silver is produced as a by-product, meaning supply cannot be ramped up quickly even as prices rise.

headline: $3-BILLION junior gold deal: G Mining Ventures buys G2 Goldfields to build Guyana's next 500koz mine
snippet: G Mining Ventures Corp. agreed to acquire adjacent G2 Goldfields Inc. in an all-share transaction valued at approximately C$3 billion, combining the fully-financed Oko West project with G2's Oko-Ghanie project in Guyana to create an operation capable of producing 500,000+ oz/year. The deal unlocks over C$1 billion in quantifiable synergies from shared infrastructure and mine sequencing, and Benzinga argues it validates junior gold companies as serious acquisition targets in the current bull cycle.

headline: UBS: GOLD will 'rally substantially' if geopolitical risk stays high while rate expectations fall
snippet: UBS analysts told Kitco News that gold is poised to rally substantially if two conditions persist simultaneously: elevated geopolitical uncertainty — currently elevated due to the Middle East conflict — and falling interest rate expectations driven by softer inflation data. The bank sees the current setup as unusually favourable for bullion, with a weaker dollar, declining real yields, and record central bank demand all aligning at the same time.

headline: URANIUM holds at $85/LB after Q1 spike to $101 — utilities rushing back to lock in term contracts
snippet: Uranium spot prices consolidated to US$85.45/lb by mid-April after peaking at US$101.41/lb in January — a pullback Investing News Network's Q1 recap attributes to speculative de-risking rather than a change in fundamentals, with utilities now actively re-entering the term market to cover uncovered demand. NexGen Energy's Rook I project received its construction licence in March, and Denison Mines' Phoenix ISR mine is also advancing, with the two projects alone representing nearly 30 million lb/year of future production.
